message:
chore(beads): close ot1z (CRITICAL ZRANGESTORE panic)

Closed via direct JSONL edit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "frankenredis-2uvy", "title": "Reject CONFIG SET io-threads-do-reads as immutable", "description": "Redis marks io-threads-do-reads as DEBUG_CONFIG | IMMUTABLE_CONFIG in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c. FrankenRedis exposes io-threads-do-reads as a static CONFIG parameter and currently accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. Add runtime and core_config conformance coverage that rejects mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-18T12:35:38.623888567Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-18T12:44:28.413316638Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-18T12:44:28.411577290Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["config", "conformance", "upstream-parity"]}

 {"id": "frankenredis-2whr", "title": "[MOCK] XREADGROUP BLOCK is still a documented non-blocking stub", "description": "Evidence: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:20153 documents "Integer BLOCK values are accepted (non-blocking stub)" in the XREADGROUP regression.

Current gap: XREADGROUP accepts BLOCK syntax but still behaves like a placeholder immediate-return path instead of real consumer-group blocking semantics.

Real impl path: implement BLOCK scheduling in the XREADGROUP handler in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs and connect it to consumer-group wakeup / pending-delivery logic in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-22T08:43:51.301068941Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-22T12:33:13.846327788Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-22T12:33:13.846327788Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
-{"id": "frankenredis-2yc4", "title": "fr-command: OBJECT ENCODING / OBJECT REFCOUNT silently accept extra trailing args", "description": "fr-command::object_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:13275 used `if argv.len() < 3` for the ENCODING and REFCOUNT subcommands instead of `!= 3`. As a result, 'OBJECT ENCODING k k2' returned an empty/unintended reply (the trailing k2 was ignored) instead of the upstream 'ERR wrong number of arguments for object|encoding' error. Fixed by tightening to !=3.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701). 'OBJECT ENCODING k k2' on fr returned an empty reply; redis returned the upstream wrong-arity error.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T05:04:38.819056720Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T05:04:55.916615Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T05:04:55.916615Z", "close_reason": "OBJECT ENCODING/REFCOUNT now reject trailing extra args via != 3 arity check, matching upstream byte-for-byte. fr-runtime latency_graph_without_samples test realigned for the ERR prefix."}
+{"id":"frankenredis-2yc4","title":"fr-command: OBJECT ENCODING / OBJECT REFCOUNT silently accept extra trailing args","description":"fr-command::object_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:13275 used `if argv.len() < 3` for the ENCODING and REFCOUNT subcommands instead of `!= 3`. As a result, 'OBJECT ENCODING k k2' returned an empty/unintended reply (the trailing k2 was ignored) instead of the upstream 'ERR wrong number of arguments for object|encoding' error. Fixed by tightening to !=3.

 {"id": "frankenredis-34b8", "title": "Add cross-process replication integration test", "description": "# Replication Integration Test

## What
An end-to-end test that starts two FrankenRedis instances, configures one as a replica, writes data to the primary, and verifies it appears on the replica.

## Why
Zero cross-process replication tests exist. This is the definitive proof that replication actually works.

## Implementation

### Test 1: Full Resync
1. Start primary on random port P1
2. SET 100 keys with various types (strings, lists, hashes, zsets) on primary
3. Start replica with --replicaof 127.0.0.1 P1 on random port P2
4. Wait for sync: poll INFO replication on replica until master_link_status:up and offset matches
5. GET all 100 keys on replica → verify each matches primary
6. Verify DBSIZE on replica matches primary

### Test 2: Live Streaming
1. After Test 1 sync completes, SET 50 more keys on primary
2. Wait 500ms for propagation
3. Verify all 50 new keys appear on replica
4. INCR a counter on primary 100 times
5. Verify counter value on replica matches

### Test 3: Partial Resync After Brief Disconnect
1. After Test 2, note current replication offset on replica
2. Kill replica connection (SHUTDOWN on replica, or just drop TCP)
3. SET 10 more keys on primary (these go into the backlog)
4. Restart replica with same --replicaof
5. Check INFO replication for sync_partial_ok increment
6. Verify 10 new keys appear on replica

### Test 4: Full Resync After Backlog Overflow
1. SET enough keys on primary to overflow the backlog (>1MB of commands)
2. Start fresh replica
3. Verify it does a FULLRESYNC (not partial)
4. Verify all data present

### Logging
- Log each phase start/end with timestamp
- Log replication offsets at each verification point
- Log sync mode (FULLRESYNC vs CONTINUE) detected
- Log data integrity check results: "Verified 100/100 keys match"

## Depends On
- All replication implementation beads (1v8b, de0t, iz1j, 1ng7)

## Files
- crates/fr-server/tests/replication_e2e.rs (new)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:17.174880997Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T19:12:49.332562863Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T19:12:49.332210336Z", "close_reason": "Added tcp_frankenredis_to_frankenredis_fullresync_and_live_streaming test. Proves: FULLRESYNC with 20 string keys + list replicated from primary to replica, live streaming of 10 additional keys after sync, INCR counter propagation (50 increments). Test passes in 2.3s.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-34b8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-1ng7", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:45.079463526Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-34b8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-1v8b", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:44.958335206Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-34b8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-de0t", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:45.201883030Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

+{"id": "frankenredis-ot1z", "title": "CRITICAL: ZRANGESTORE z2 z 0 -1 BYSCORE panics fr-server (BTreeMap range start > end)", "description": "Calling 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' against fr-server triggers a panic that closes the connection (and presumably tears down the worker thread):

  thread 'main' panicked at .../btree/search.rs:121:21:
  range start is greater than range end in BTreeMap

This happens because the BYSCORE flag interprets the third+fourth args as score bounds; passing 0 and -1 yields start=0, end=-1, and the BTreeMap::range() call inside the score-range walker panics rather than returning an empty range.

Acceptance: 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' returns Integer(0) (empty range, dest unchanged) byte-for-byte matching vendored redis 7.2.4. Same fix should cover ZRANGE BYSCORE / ZRANGEBYSCORE with start > end: those already return empty arrays without panicking, but the underlying BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE may take a different path that's not guarded.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) caught the panic. Affects ANY caller that hits start > end on the BYSCORE/BYLEX-backed BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE.

Severity rationale: fr-server panic + connection close from a single client command is a denial-of-service vector. Promoting to P1.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T05:05:52.236811396Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T05:14:08.673050Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T05:14:08.673050Z", "close_reason": "CRITICAL panic from ZRANGESTORE-BYSCORE-with-inverted-range fixed: store::zrangebyscore[_withscores] now early-returns empty when min > max instead of feeding the inverted bounds to BTreeMap::range. fr-server stays alive across the previously-fatal command. Verified byte-for-byte against vendored redis 7.2.4."}

 {"id": "frankenredis-ozcx", "title": "[HIGH] RESP3 map reply type '%' missing in fr-protocol parser (HELLO 3 path)", "description": "Surfaced by live_redis_core_connection_matches_runtime: hello_resp3 case sends HELLO 3 which makes upstream switch to RESP3 map replies (prefix '%'). Our fr-protocol parse_frame_internal returns RespParseError::UnsupportedResp3Type(b'%'). Fix: add Map/Set/Bool/Double/Null/BigNumber/Attribute/Push RESP3 types to fr-protocol, gated so RESP2 clients still parse the pre-HELLO stream normally.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T08:45:44.562243012Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T22:52:13.632194801Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T22:52:13.630134660Z", "close_reason": "Matrix live-oracle test passing 100% under STRICT mode. Wiring done + known-divergent cases filtered or canonicalized.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-ozj0", "title": "[HIGH] SINTERCARD numkeys/LIMIT error wording drift from upstream", "description": "Surfaced by live_redis_core_set_matches_runtime: sintercard_wrong_numkeys + sintercard_numkeys_{zero,negative} emit 'ERR numkeys can't be non-positive value' vs upstream 'ERR numkeys should be greater than 0'. sintercard_numkeys_too_large emits 'ERR syntax error' vs upstream 'ERR Number of keys can't be greater than number of args'. sintercard_limit_non_numeric emits 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' vs upstream 'ERR LIMIT can't be negative'. Fix in fr-command SINTERCARD parser.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "codex-ozj0", "created_at": "2026-04-24T04:18:18.095071760Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T22:06:38.017004313Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T22:06:38.015107783Z", "close_reason": "Validated existing SINTERCARD parser and core_set fixture/live-oracle parity; no code change needed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
